task_completion.description: Locate the LUNAR_ROVER robot in the Webots scene tree or world file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Webots Scene Tree (left panel) shows the 'LUNAR_ROVER' node selected or expanded, or a text editor shows the 'DEF LUNAR_ROVER Robot' block.

task_completion.description: Configure the LUNAR_ROVER battery field to [10000, 36000, 50].
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Webots Field Editor/Scene Tree or a text editor, the 'battery' field for LUNAR_ROVER is visible and explicitly set to the values 10000, 36000, and 50.
The Scene Tree shows a LightSensor node with the name 'solar_panel' selected or expanded, or a text editor shows the 'DEF solar_panel LightSensor' block.

task_completion.description: Set the solar_panel rotation to '0 1 0 -1.5708' and fieldOfView to 3.1415.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'rotation' field is set to '0 1 0 -1.5708' and the 'fieldOfView' field is set to '3.1415' within the solar_panel LightSensor properties.
The 'lookupTable' field within the solar_panel LightSensor properties is set to contain the values [0 0 0] and [1361 50 0].

A 'Save World As' dialog in Webots, a 'Save As' dialog in a text editor, or terminal commands showing the file being saved or copied to '/home/ga/Desktop/lunar_power_sim.wbt'. The Webots window title may also update to reflect the new filename.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Webots application or a genuine text editor to modify the world file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Webots GUI or a text editor (like VS Code, nano, vim) open with the .wbt file, rather than just echoing strings directly into a file from the terminal.

integrity.description: The agent modified the existing LUNAR_ROVER and solar_panel nodes rather than creating dummy nodes.
integrity.visual_evidence: The modifications should be seen within the context of the existing complex robot definition, not as isolated, newly appended text at the bottom of the file.

integrity.description: The file was genuinely saved to the requested path.
integrity.visual_evidence: Visual confirmation of the save action via GUI menus (File -> Save World As) or standard editor save commands, rather than just a mocked terminal output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The LUNAR_ROVER battery must be exactly [10000.0, 36000.0, 50.0]. The 'solar_panel' LightSensor must have rotation [0.0, 1.0, 0.0, -1.5708], fieldOfView 3.1415, and lookupTable [0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 1361.0, 50.0, 0.0]. The final world file must be saved to /home/ga/Desktop/lunar_power_sim.wbt.
